   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i></i></b></p><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31" name="us-gaap:ResearchAndDevelopmentExpensePolicy" id="c56491383" escape="true"><p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i>Research and Development </i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">Costs incurred in research and development are expensed as incurred.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i>&#xa0;</i></b></p></ix:nonNumeric><p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i></i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i></i></b></p><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31" name="us-gaap:StandardProductWarrantyPolicy" id="c56491384" escape="true"><p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i>Warranty Reserve</i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">The Company generally provides&#xa0;<span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c56482692">one</span>&#xa0;to&#xa0;<span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c56482693">two</span>-year warranties against defects in materials and workmanship and will either repair the products, provide replacements at&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">no</em>&#xa0;charge to customers or issue a refund. As they are considered assurance-type warranties, the Company does&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em>&#xa0;account for them as separate performance obligations. Warranty reserve requirements are generally based on a specific assessment of the products sold with warranties when a customer asserts a claim for warranty or a product defect.&#xa0;See Note <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">13</em>&#xa0;for the changes in warranty reserves for the periods presented.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i>&#xa0;</i></b></p></ix:nonNumeric><p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i></i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i></i></b></p><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31" name="us-gaap:LesseeLeasesPolicyTextBlock" id="c56491385" escape="true"><p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i>Leases</i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">The Company determines if an arrangement is a lease at inception. Lease terms <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em>include options to extend or terminate the lease when it is reasonably certain that the Company will exercise that option. Operating lease right-of-use (&#x201c;ROU&#x201d;) assets represent the Company&#x2019;s right to use an underlying asset for the lease term and operating lease liabilities represent the Company&#x2019;s obligation to make lease payments arising from the lease. Operating lease ROU assets and liabilities are recognized at the commencement date based on the present value of remaining lease payments over the lease term. ROU assets also include any initial direct costs incurred and prepaid lease payments, less lease incentives received. Because the implicit rate in each lease is&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em>&#xa0;readily determinable, the Company uses its estimated incremental borrowing rate to determine the present value of the remaining lease payment. The Company recognizes operating lease costs on a straight-line basis over the lease term.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">The Company does&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em>&#xa0;record short-term leases with a term of&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">12</em>&#xa0;months or less at the commencement date on the Consolidated Balance Sheets. For lease arrangements that contain lease and non-lease components, the Company accounts for them as single lease components.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">For lease arrangements where the Company is the lessor, the Company recognizes lease income from operating leases on a straight-line basis over the lease term.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i>&#xa0;</i></b></p></ix:nonNumeric><p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i></i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i></i></b></p><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationOptionAndIncentivePlansPolicy" id="c56491386" escape="true"><p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i>Stock-Based Compensation </i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">The Company measures the cost of employee services received in exchange for an award of equity instruments based on the grant-date fair value of the award.<b><i>&#xa0;</i></b>The fair value of RSUs with service conditions or performance conditions is based on the grant date stock price. The fair value of shares issued under the ESPP and RSUs with a purchase price feature is estimated using the Black-Scholes model. The fair value of RSUs with market conditions, as well as RSUs containing both market and performance conditions, is estimated using a Monte Carlo simulation model.&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: center;">&#xa0;&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">Compensation expense related to awards with service conditions is recorded on a straight-line basis over the requisite service period. Compensation expense related to awards subject to market or performance conditions is recognized over the requisite service period for each separately vesting tranche. For awards with only market conditions, compensation expense is <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> reversed if the market conditions are <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> satisfied. For awards with performance conditions, as well as awards containing both market and performance conditions, the Company recognizes compensation expense when the performance goals are achieved, or when it becomes probable that the performance goals will be achieved. Management performs the probability assessment on a quarterly basis by reviewing external factors, such as macroeconomic conditions and the analog industry revenue forecasts, and internal factors, such as the Company&#x2019;s business and operational&#xa0;objectives and revenue forecasts. Changes in the probability assessment of achievement of the performance conditions are accounted for in the period of change by recording a cumulative catch-up adjustment as if the new estimate had been applied since the service inception date. Any previously recognized compensation expense is reversed if the performance conditions are <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> expected to be satisfied as a result of management&#x2019;s assessment.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">The Company accounts for forfeitures of equity awards when they occur.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;&#xa0;</p></ix:nonNumeric><p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i></i></b></p><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31" name="us-gaap:IncomeTaxPolicyTextBlock" id="c56491387" escape="true"><p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i>Accounting for Income Taxes</i></b>&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">The Company recognizes federal, state and foreign current tax liabilities or assets based on its estimate of taxes payable or refundable in the current fiscal year by tax jurisdiction. The Company also recognizes federal, state and foreign deferred tax assets or liabilities for its estimate of future tax effects attributable to temporary differences and carryforwards. The Company records a valuation allowance to reduce any deferred tax assets by the amount of any tax benefits that, based on available evidence and judgment, are <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> expected to be realized.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: center;">&#xa0;</p>
   <div class="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
    <div class="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-size: 10pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">
     <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"></em></div></div><ix:exclude><div class="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt"><div class="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-size: 10pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">54</em>
    </div>
    <hr style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; border: none; width: 100%; height: 2px; color: #000000; background-color: #000000" />
    <div class="PGHDR" style="text-align: left; width: 100%">
     <a href="#toc" style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt">Table of Contents</a>
    </div>
   </div>
   <p style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left">&#xa0;</p></ix:exclude><p style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left"></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">The Company&#x2019;s calculation of current and deferred tax assets and liabilities is based on certain estimates and judgments and involves dealing with uncertainties in the application of complex tax laws. The Company&#x2019;s estimates of current and deferred tax assets and liabilities <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em>change based, in part, on added certainty, finality or uncertainty to an anticipated outcome, changes in accounting or tax laws in the U.S. or foreign jurisdictions where the Company operates, or changes in other facts or circumstances. In addition, the Company recognizes liabilities for potential U.S. and foreign income tax for uncertain income tax positions taken on its tax returns if it has less than a <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">50%</em> likelihood of being sustained. If the Company determines that payment of these amounts is unnecessary or if the recorded tax liability is less than its current assessment, the Company <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em>be required to recognize an income tax benefit or additional income tax expense in its financial statements in the period such determination is made. The Company has calculated its uncertain tax positions which were attributable to certain estimates and judgments.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i>&#xa0;</i></b>&#xa0;</p></ix:nonNumeric><p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i></i></b></p><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31" name="mpwr:LitigationAndContingenciesPolicyPolicyTextBlock" id="c56491388" escape="true"><p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i>Litigation and Contingencies</i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">The Company is a party to actions and proceedings in the ordinary course of business, including potential litigation regarding its stockholders and its intellectual property, challenges to the enforceability or validity of its intellectual property, claims that the Company&#x2019;s products infringe on the intellectual property rights of others, and employment matters. The pending proceedings involve complex questions of fact and law and will require the expenditure of significant funds and the diversion of other resources to prosecute and defend. In addition, from time to time, the Company becomes aware that it is subject to other contingent liabilities.&#xa0;When this occurs, the Company will evaluate the appropriate accounting for the potential contingent liabilities to determine whether a contingent liability should be recorded. In making this determination, management <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may, </em>depending on the nature of the matter, consult with internal and external legal counsel and technical experts. Based on the facts and circumstances in each matter, the Company uses its judgment to determine whether it is probable that a contingent loss has occurred and whether the amount of such loss can be estimated. If the Company determines a loss is probable and estimable, the Company records a contingent loss. In determining the amount of a contingent loss, the Company takes into account advice received from experts for each specific matter regarding the status of legal proceedings, settlement negotiations, prior case history and other factors. Should the judgments and estimates made by management need to be adjusted as additional information becomes available, the Company <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may </em>need to record additional contingent losses. Alternatively, if the judgments and estimates made by management are adjusted, for example, if a particular contingent loss does <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> occur, the contingent loss recorded would be reversed.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">Litigation expense recorded on the Consolidated Statements of Operations includes primarily patent infringement litigation and other business matters. The Company records litigation costs in the period in which they are incurred. Proceeds resulting from settlement of litigation or favorable judgments are recorded as a reduction against litigation expense.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i>&#xa0;</i></b></p></ix:nonNumeric><p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i></i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i></i></b></p><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31" name="us-gaap:EarningsPerSharePolicyTextBlock" id="c56491389" escape="true"><p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i>Net Income per Share</i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i>&#xa0;</i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">Basic net income per share is computed by dividing net income by the weighted-average number of common shares outstanding for the period. Diluted net income per share reflects the potential dilution that would occur if outstanding securities or other contracts to issue common stock were exercised or converted into common shares, and calculated using the treasury stock method.&#xa0;Contingently issuable shares, including equity awards with performance conditions or market conditions, are considered outstanding common shares and included in the basic net income per share as of the date that all necessary conditions to earn the awards have been satisfied. Prior to the end of the contingency period, the number of contingently issuable shares included in the diluted net income per share is based on the number of shares, if any, that would be issuable under the terms of the arrangement at the end of the reporting period.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">The Company&#x2019;s RSUs contain forfeitable rights to receive cash dividend equivalents, which are accumulated and paid to the employees when the underlying RSUs vest. Dividend equivalents accumulated on the underlying RSUs are forfeited if the employees do&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em>&#xa0;fulfill the requisite service requirement and, as a result, the awards do&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em>&#xa0;vest. Accordingly, these awards are&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em>&#xa0;treated as participating securities in the net income per share calculation.&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i>&#xa0;</i></b></p></ix:nonNumeric><p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i></i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i></i></b></p><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31" name="us-gaap:ComprehensiveIncomePolicyPolicyTextBlock" id="c56491390" escape="true"><p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i>Comprehensive Income&#xa0;</i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i>&#xa0;</i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">Comprehensive income represents the change in the Company&#x2019;s net assets during the period from non-owner sources. Accumulated other comprehensive loss&#xa0;presented on the Consolidated Balance Sheets primarily consists of unrealized gains or losses related to available-for-sale investments and foreign currency translation adjustments.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p></ix:nonNumeric><p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"></p><ix:exclude><p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"></p>
   <div class="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
    <div class="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-size: 10pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">
     <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">55</em>
    </div>
    <hr style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; border: none; width: 100%; height: 2px; color: #000000; background-color: #000000" />
    <div class="PGHDR" style="text-align: left; width: 100%">
     <a href="#toc" style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt">Table of Contents</a>
    </div>
   </div>
   <p style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left">&#xa0;</p></ix:exclude><p style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left"></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i></i></b></p><ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31" name="us-gaap:NewAccountingPronouncementsPolicyPolicyTextBlock" id="c56491391" escape="true"><p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i>Recently A</i></b><b><i>dopted Accounting Pronouncement</i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">In&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> February 2016,&#xa0;</em>the FASB issued Accounting Standards Update (&#x201c;ASU&#x201d;)&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">No.</em>&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em>-<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">02,</em>&#xa0;<i>Leases (Topic&#xa0;</i><i><em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">842</em></i><i>),</i>&#xa0;which requires entities to recognize ROU&#xa0;assets and lease liabilities on the balance sheets for leases with terms greater than&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">12</em>&#xa0;months.&#xa0;In addition, the standard applies to leases embedded in service or other arrangements. The Company adopted the standard on&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 1, 2019&#xa0;</em>using the modified retrospective transition method and did&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em>&#xa0;restate comparative periods, as permitted by the standard. In addition, the Company elected the transition practical expedients to&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> reassess whether its outstanding contracts as of <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2018 </em>contained or were leases, classification of its existing leases and lease terms.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">Upon adoption, the Company recognized ROU assets and lease liabilities of its outstanding operating leases on the Consolidated Balance Sheets, primarily related to real estate. The adoption did&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em>&#xa0;have a material&#xa0;impact on the Consolidated Statements of Operations or the Consolidated Statements of Cash Flows. See Note&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">7</em>&#xa0;for further discussion.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;"><b><i>&#xa0;</i></b>&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i>Recent Accounting Pronouncements <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">Not</em>&#xa0;Yet Adopted as of <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2019</em></i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">In <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 2019, </em>the FASB issued ASU <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">No.</em> <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2019</em>-<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">12,</em> <i>Income Taxes (Topic <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">740</em>): Simplifying the Accounting for Income Taxes</i><i>,</i> which eliminates certain exceptions related to the approach for intra-period tax allocation, the methodology for calculating income taxes in an interim period and the recognition of deferred tax liabilities for outside basis differences. The standard also clarifies and simplifies other aspects of the accounting for income taxes.&#xa0;The standard will be effective for annual reporting periods beginning after <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 15, 2020. </em>Early adoption is permitted. The standard will generally be applied prospectively, with certain exceptions. The Company is evaluating the impact of the adoption on its consolidated financial statements.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">In&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> August 2018,&#xa0;</em>the FASB issued ASU&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">No.</em>&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018</em>-<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">13,</em>&#xa0;<i>Fair Value Measurement (Topic</i>&#xa0;<i><em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">820</em></i>):<i> Disclosure Framework &#x2014; Changes to the Disclosure Requirements for Fair Value Measurement,&#xa0;</i>which changes certain disclosure requirements, including those related to Level&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">3</em>&#xa0;fair value measurements. The standard will be effective for annual reporting periods beginning after&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 15, 2019.&#xa0;</em>The Company does&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em>&#xa0;expect the adoption of this standard to have a material impact on its disclosures.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">In&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 2017,&#xa0;</em>the FASB issued ASU&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">No.</em>&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017</em>-<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">04,</em>&#xa0;<i>Intangibles &#x2013; Goodwill and Other&#xa0;(Topic&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">350</em>): Simplifying the Test for Goodwill Impairment,&#xa0;</i>which simplifies the accounting for goodwill impairment. The guidance removes step&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">two</em>&#xa0;of the goodwill impairment test, which requires a hypothetical purchase price allocation to measure the amount of a goodwill impairment. A goodwill impairment will now be the amount by which a reporting unit&#x2019;s carrying value exceeds its fair value,&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em>&#xa0;to exceed the carrying amount of goodwill. Entities will continue to have the option to perform a qualitative assessment to determine if a quantitative impairment test is necessary. The standard will be applied prospectively, and will be effective for annual reporting periods beginning after&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 15, 2019.&#xa0;</em>The Company will continue to perform the qualitative assessment on its goodwill impairment test and does&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em>&#xa0;expect the adoption of this standard to have a material impact.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">In&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> June 2016,&#xa0;</em>the FASB issued ASU&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">No.</em>&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2016</em>-<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">13,</em>&#xa0;<i>Financial Instruments &#x2013; Credit Losses (Topic&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">326</em>):&#xa0;Measurement of Credit Losses on Financial Instruments,&#xa0;</i>which introduces a model based on expected losses to estimate credit losses for financial assets held at amortized cost. In addition, for available-for-sale debt securities, the standard eliminates the concept of other-than-temporary impairment and entities will be required to recognize an allowance for credit losses rather than reductions in the amortized cost of the securities. The standard will be effective for annual reporting periods beginning after&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 15, 2019.&#xa0;</em>Entities will apply the standard by recording a cumulative-effect adjustment to retained earnings. The effect of the standard on the Company&#x2019;s available-for-sale debt securities will largely depend on the composition of the investment portfolio, the financial condition of the issuers, the credit quality and expected collectability of the securities, and economic conditions.&#xa0;The Company does <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> expect the standard to have a material impact on its consolidated financial statements at the time of adoption.</p></ix:nonNumeric><p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"></p>
  </ix:nonNumeric>
  <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b>&#xa0;</b>&#xa0;<b>&#xa0;</b>&#xa0;&#xa0;</p>
  <div>
    &#xa0;
  </div>
  <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31" name="us-gaap:RevenueFromContractWithCustomerTextBlock" id="c1442954" escape="true">
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2.</em> REVENUE RECOGNITION</b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b>&#xa0;</b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;">The Company adopted ASU&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">No.</em>&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em>-<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">09,</em>&#xa0;<i>Revenue from Contracts with Customers (Topic&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">606</em>), </i>on&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January 1, 2018&#xa0;</em>using the modified retrospective transition method applied to those contracts which were&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em>&#xa0;completed as of&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017.&#xa0;</em>Results for reporting periods beginning after&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> January&#xa0;</em><em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1,</em>&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018</em>&#xa0;are presented under Topic&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">606,</em>&#xa0;while prior-period amounts have&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em>&#xa0;been retrospectively adjusted and were reported in accordance with Topic&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">605,</em>&#xa0;<i>Revenue Recognition</i>.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;"><b><i>&#xa0;</i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: justify;"><b><i>Revenue from Product Sales</i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b>&#xa0;</b>&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">The Company generates revenue primarily&#xa0;from product sales, which include assembled and tested integrated circuits, as well as dies in wafer form. These product sales were&#xa0;<ix:nonFraction id="c56482779" contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31_ProductOrServiceAxis-ProductMember" unitRef="Pure" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="2" name="mpwr:PercentageOfRevenue" scale="-2">99</ix:nonFraction>%, <ix:nonFraction id="c56482780" contextRef="d_2018-01-01_2018-12-31_ProductOrServiceAxis-ProductMember" unitRef="Pure" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="2" name="mpwr:PercentageOfRevenue" scale="-2">98</ix:nonFraction>% and&#xa0;<ix:nonFraction id="c56482781" contextRef="d_2017-01-01_2017-12-31_ProductOrServiceAxis-ProductMember" unitRef="Pure" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="2" name="mpwr:PercentageOfRevenue" scale="-2">98</ix:nonFraction>% of the Company&#x2019;s total revenue for the years ended <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2019, </em><em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018</em> and <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017,</em> respectively. The remaining revenue primarily includes royalty revenue&#xa0;from licensing arrangements and revenue from wafer testing services performed for&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">third</em>&#xa0;parties, which have&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em>&#xa0;been significant in all periods presented. See Note&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">16</em>&#xa0;for the disaggregation of the Company&#x2019;s revenue by geographic regions and by product families.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p><ix:exclude><p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"></p>
   <div class="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
    <div class="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-size: 10pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">
     <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">56</em>
    </div>
    <hr style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; border: none; width: 100%; height: 2px; color: #000000; background-color: #000000" />
    <div class="PGHDR" style="text-align: left; width: 100%">
     <a href="#toc" style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt">Table of Contents</a>
    </div>
   </div>
   <p style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left">&#xa0;</p></ix:exclude><p style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left"></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">The Company sells its products primarily through&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">third</em>-party distributors, value-added resellers, OEMs, ODMs and EMS&#xa0;providers. For the years ended <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2019, </em><em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018</em> and <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2017,</em> <ix:nonFraction id="c56482791" contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31_ConcentrationRiskByBenchmarkAxis-SalesRevenueNetMember_ConcentrationRiskByTypeAxis-CustomerConcentrationRiskMember_MajorCustomersAxis-DistributorSalesMember" unitRef="Pure" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="2" name="us-gaap:ConcentrationRiskPercentage1" scale="-2">83</ix:nonFraction>%, <ix:nonFraction id="c56482792" contextRef="d_2018-01-01_2018-12-31_ConcentrationRiskByBenchmarkAxis-SalesRevenueNetMember_ConcentrationRiskByTypeAxis-CustomerConcentrationRiskMember_MajorCustomersAxis-DistributorSalesMember" unitRef="Pure" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="2" name="us-gaap:ConcentrationRiskPercentage1" scale="-2">87</ix:nonFraction>% and&#xa0;<ix:nonFraction id="c56482793" contextRef="d_2017-01-01_2017-12-31_ConcentrationRiskByBenchmarkAxis-SalesRevenueNetMember_ConcentrationRiskByTypeAxis-CustomerConcentrationRiskMember_MajorCustomersAxis-DistributorSalesMember" unitRef="Pure" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="2" name="us-gaap:ConcentrationRiskPercentage1" scale="-2">88</ix:nonFraction>%, respectively, of the Company&#x2019;s product sales were made through distribution arrangements. These distribution arrangements contain enforceable rights and obligations specific to those distributors and&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em>&#xa0;the end customers. Purchase orders, which are generally governed by sales agreements or&#xa0;the Company's standard terms of sale, set the final terms for unit price, quantity, shipping and payment agreed by both parties. The Company considers purchase orders to be the&#xa0;contracts with customers.&#xa0;The unit price as stated on the purchase orders&#xa0;is considered the observable, stand-alone selling price for the arrangements.</p>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:justify;">&#xa0;&#xa0;&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">The Company recognizes revenue when it satisfies a performance obligation by transferring control of the promised goods or services to its customers, in an amount that reflects the consideration the Company expects to be entitled to in exchange for those goods or services. The Company excludes&#xa0;taxes assessed by government authorities,&#xa0;such as sales taxes, from revenue.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">Product sales consist of a single performance obligation that the Company satisfies at a point in time. The Company recognizes product revenue from distributors and direct end customers when the following events have occurred: (a) the Company has transferred physical possession of the products, (b) the Company has a present right to payment, (c) the customer has legal title to the products, and (d) the customer bears significant risks and rewards of ownership of the products. In accordance with the shipping terms specified in the contracts, these criteria are generally met when the products are shipped from the Company&#x2019;s facilities (such as the &#x201c;Ex Works&#x201d; shipping term) or delivered to the customers&#x2019; locations (such as the &#x201c;Delivered Duty Paid&#x201d; shipping term).</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">Under certain consignment agreements, revenue is&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em>&#xa0;recognized when the products are shipped and delivered to be held at customers&#x2019; designated locations&#xa0;because the Company continues to control the products and retain ownership, and the customers do&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em>&#xa0;have an unconditional obligation to pay. The Company recognizes revenue when the customers consume&#xa0;the products from the consigned inventory&#xa0;locations or, in some cases, after a&#xa0;<ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31" name="mpwr:LapsePeriodForConsignment" id="c56482797" format="ixt-sec:durday">60</ix:nonNumeric>-day period from the delivery date has passed, at which time control&#xa0;transfers to the customers and the Company invoices them for payment.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i>Variable Consideration</i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">The Company accounts for price adjustment and stock rotation rights as variable consideration that reduces the transaction price, and recognizes that reduction in the same period the associated revenue is recognized.&#xa0;Three U.S.-based distributors have price adjustment rights when they sell the Company&#x2019;s products to their end customers at a price that is lower than the distribution price invoiced by the Company. When the Company receives claims from the distributors that products have been sold to the end customers at the lower price, the Company issues the distributors credit memos for the price adjustments. The Company estimates the price adjustments using the expected value method based on an analysis of&#xa0;historical&#xa0;claims, at both the distributor and product level, as well as an assessment of any known trends of product sales mix.&#xa0;Other U.S.&#xa0;distributors and non-U.S.&#xa0;distributors, which make up the majority of the Company&#x2019;s total sales to distributors, do&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em>&#xa0;have price adjustment rights.&#xa0;The Company records a credit against accounts receivable for the estimated price adjustments, with a corresponding reduction to revenue.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">Certain distributors&#xa0;have limited stock rotation rights that permit the return of a small percentage of the previous&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">six</em>&#xa0;months&#x2019; purchases in accordance with the contract terms. The Company estimates the stock rotation returns using the expected value method based on an analysis of historical returns, and the current level of inventory in the distribution channel.&#xa0;The Company records a liability for the stock rotation reserve, with a corresponding reduction to revenue.&#xa0;In addition, the Company recognizes an asset for product returns&#xa0;which represents&#xa0;the right to recover products from the customers&#xa0;related to stock rotations, with a corresponding reduction to cost of revenue.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b>&#xa0;</b>&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i>Contract Balances</i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">The Company records a receivable when it has an unconditional right to receive consideration after the performance obligations are satisfied. As of&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2019 </em>and <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018,</em>&#xa0;accounts receivable totaled&#xa0;$<ix:nonFraction id="c56482801" contextRef="i_2019-12-31" unitRef="USD" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5" name="us-gaap:AccountsReceivableNetCurrent" scale="6">52.7</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million and&#xa0;$<ix:nonFraction id="c56482802" contextRef="i_2018-12-31" unitRef="USD" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5" name="us-gaap:AccountsReceivableNetCurrent" scale="6">55.2</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million, respectively. The Company did&#xa0;<span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c56482803"><span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c56482804">not</span></span>&#xa0;record any allowance for doubtful accounts&#xa0;as of&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2019 </em>and <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018.</em></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">For certain customers located in Asia, the Company requires cash payments&#xa0;<span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c56482806">two</span>&#xa0;weeks before the products are scheduled to be shipped to the customers. The Company records these payments received in advance of performance as customer prepayments within current accrued liabilities. As of&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2019 </em>and <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018,</em>&#xa0;customer prepayments totaled&#xa0;$<ix:nonFraction id="c56482808" contextRef="i_2019-12-31_BalanceSheetLocationAxis-AccruedLiabilitiesCurrentMember_MajorCustomersAxis-CertainCustomersInChinaMember" unitRef="USD" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5" name="us-gaap:ContractWithCustomerLiabilityCurrent" scale="6">3.4</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million and&#xa0;$<ix:nonFraction id="c56482809" contextRef="i_2018-12-31_BalanceSheetLocationAxis-AccruedLiabilitiesCurrentMember_MajorCustomersAxis-CertainCustomersInChinaMember" unitRef="USD" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5" name="us-gaap:ContractWithCustomerLiabilityCurrent" scale="6">2.5</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million, respectively. The increase in the customer prepayment balance for the&#xa0;year ended&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2019&#xa0;</em>resulted from an increase in unfulfilled customer orders for which the Company has received payments. For the&#xa0;year ended <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2019,&#xa0;</em>the Company recognized&#xa0;$<ix:nonFraction id="c56482810" contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31_MajorCustomersAxis-CertainCustomersInChinaMember" unitRef="USD" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5" name="us-gaap:ContractWithCustomerLiabilityRevenueRecognized" scale="6">2.5</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million of revenue that was included in the customer prepayment balance as of&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2018.</em></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b>&#xa0;</b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i>Practical Expedients</i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">The Company has elected the&#xa0;practical expedient to expense sales commissions as incurred because the amortization period would have been&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em>&#xa0;year or less.&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <div class="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
    <div class="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-size: 10pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">
     <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"></em></div></div><ix:exclude><div class="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt"><div class="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-size: 10pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">57</em>
    </div>
    <hr style="PAGE-BREAK-AFTER: always; border: none; width: 100%; height: 2px; color: #000000; background-color: #000000" />

     <a href="#toc" style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt">Table of Contents</a>

   <p style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left">&#xa0;</p></ix:exclude><p style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left"></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">The Company&#x2019;s standard payment terms generally require customers to pay&#xa0;<ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31_RangeAxis-MinimumMember" name="mpwr:ContractWithCustomerPaymentTerm" id="c56482813" format="ixt-sec:durday">30</ix:nonNumeric>&#xa0;to&#xa0;<ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31_RangeAxis-MaximumMember" name="mpwr:ContractWithCustomerPaymentTerm" id="c56482814" format="ixt-sec:durday">60</ix:nonNumeric>&#xa0;days after the Company satisfies the performance obligations. For those customers who are required to pay in advance, the Company satisfies the performance obligations generally&#xa0;within&#xa0;<span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c56482815">two</span>&#xa0;weeks. The Company has elected&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em>&#xa0;to determine whether contacts with customers contain significant financing components.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">The Company&#x2019;s unsatisfied performance obligations primarily include&#xa0;products held in consignment arrangements and customer purchase orders for products that the Company has&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em>&#xa0;yet shipped. Because the Company expects to fulfill these performance obligations within&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em>&#xa0;year, the Company has elected&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em>&#xa0;to disclose the amount of these remaining performance obligations or the timing of recognition.</p>
  </ix:nonNumeric>
  <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
  <div>
    &#xa0;
  </div>
  <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31" name="mpwr:CashCashEquivalentsInvestmentsAndRestrictedCashDisclosureTextBlock" id="c1442955" escape="true">
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">3.</em>&#xa0;&#xa0;CASH, CASH EQUIVALENTS, INVESTMENTS AND RESTRICTED CASH</b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">The following is a summary of the Company&#x2019;s cash, cash equivalents and investments (in thousands):&#xa0;</p>

   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">As of&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December&#xa0;</em><em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">31,</em>&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2019</em> and <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018,</em>&#xa0;unrealized losses that had been in a continuous loss position for&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">12</em>&#xa0;months or longer were&#xa0;$<ix:nonFraction id="c56482830" contextRef="i_2019-12-31" unitRef="USD" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5" name="us-gaap:DebtSecuritiesAvailableForSaleContinuousUnrealizedLossPosition12MonthsOrLongerAccumulatedLoss" scale="6">0.2</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million&#xa0;and&#xa0;$<ix:nonFraction id="c56482831" contextRef="i_2018-12-31" unitRef="USD" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5" name="us-gaap:DebtSecuritiesAvailableForSaleContinuousUnrealizedLossPosition12MonthsOrLongerAccumulatedLoss" scale="6">1.6</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million, respectively. The Company typically invests in highly rated securities, with the primary objective of minimizing the potential risk of principal loss. The Company&#x2019;s investment policy generally requires securities to be investment grade and limits the amount of credit exposure to any <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">one</em> issuer. When evaluating a debt security for other-than-temporary impairment, management reviewed factors such as the Company&#x2019;s intent to sell, or whether it will more likely than <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> be required to sell, the security before recovery of its amortized cost basis, the extent to which the fair value of the security is less than its cost, the financial condition of the issuer and the credit quality of the investment. As of&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2019 </em>and <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018,</em>&#xa0;the Company did&#xa0;<span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c56482834">not</span>&#xa0;consider any of its available-for-sale investments to be other-than-temporarily impaired.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">The Company redeemed $<ix:nonFraction id="c56482835" contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31_FinancialInstrumentAxis-AuctionRateSecuritiesMember" unitRef="USD" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5" name="mpwr:RedemptionAuctionRateSecurities" scale="6">0.3</ix:nonFraction> million and $<ix:nonFraction id="c56482836" contextRef="d_2018-01-01_2018-12-31_FinancialInstrumentAxis-AuctionRateSecuritiesMember" unitRef="USD" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-6" name="mpwr:RedemptionAuctionRateSecurities" scale="6">2.0</ix:nonFraction> million of its auction-rate securities at face value for the years ended <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2019 </em>and <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018,</em> respectively. There were <span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c56482838">no</span> sales&#xa0;of auction-rate securities for the year ended <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2017. </em>The underlying maturities of the remaining auction-rate securities are up to <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31" name="mpwr:AuctionRateSecuritiesMaturities" id="c56482839" format="ixt-sec:duryear">27</ix:nonNumeric> years. As of <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2019 </em>and <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2018,</em> the impairment of $<ix:nonFraction id="c56482841" contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31_FinancialInstrumentAxis-AuctionRateSecuritiesMember" unitRef="USD" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5" name="us-gaap:ImpairmentOfInvestments" scale="6">0.2</ix:nonFraction> million and $<ix:nonFraction id="c56482842" contextRef="d_2018-01-01_2018-12-31_FinancialInstrumentAxis-AuctionRateSecuritiesMember" unitRef="USD" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5" name="us-gaap:ImpairmentOfInvestments" scale="6">0.3</ix:nonFraction> million, respectively, was determined to be temporary because management possessed both the intent and ability to hold these securities for a period of time sufficient to allow for any anticipated recovery in fair value. In addition, management believed that it was more likely than <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> that the Company would <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em> have to sell these securities before recovery of its amortized cost basis. All scheduled interest payments have been received on time pursuant to the terms and conditions of the securities. To date, the Company has redeemed $<ix:nonFraction id="c56482845" contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31" unitRef="USD" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-7" name="mpwr:RedemptionAuctionRateSecuritiesToDate" scale="6">40.0</ix:nonFraction> million, or <ix:nonFraction id="c56482846" contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31" unitRef="Pure" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="2" name="mpwr:PercentageOfAuctionRateSecuritiesRedeemedatParValue" scale="-2">92</ix:nonFraction>% of the original portfolio in these auction-rate securities, at par without any realized losses.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b>&#xa0;</b>&#xa0;</p><ix:exclude><p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"></p>
   <div class="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">
    <div class="PGNUM" style="text-align: center; width: 100%; font-size: 10pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">
     <em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">59</em>

   <p style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left">&#xa0;</p></ix:exclude><p style="font-family: 'Times New Roman', Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left"></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i>Restricted Cash</i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i>&#xa0;</i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">The following table provides a reconciliation of cash, cash equivalents and restricted cash reported on the Consolidated Balance Sheets to the amounts reported on&#xa0;the Consolidated Statements of Cash Flows (in thousands):&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31" name="us-gaap:ScheduleOfRestrictedCashAndCashEquivalentsTextBlock" id="c1442961" escape="true">
    <table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" class="finTable" style="width: 100%; font-size: 10pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; text-indent: 0px;">
     <tbody>
      <tr style="vertical-align: bottom;">

       <td style="width: 9%; text-align: right; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom: 3px double rgb(0, 0, 0);"><ix:nonFraction id="c56483011" contextRef="i_2018-12-31_FairValueByFairValueHierarchyLevelAxis-FairValueInputsLevel3Member" unitRef="USD" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-3" name="us-gaap:AssetsFairValueDisclosure" scale="3">3,241</ix:nonFraction></td>
       <td style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; padding-bottom: 3px; margin-left: 0pt;">&#xa0;</td>
      </tr>
     </tbody>
    </table>
   </ix:continuation>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="margin: 0pt;">_______________</p>
   <table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" style="width: 100%; text-indent: 0px;">
    <tbody>
     <tr style="vertical-align: top;">
      <td style="width: 18pt;"> <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&#x25cf;</p> </td>
      <td> <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt;">Level&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">1&#x2014;includes</em>&#xa0;instruments with quoted prices in active markets for identical assets.</p> </td>
     </tr>
     <tr style="vertical-align: top;">
      <td style="width: 18pt;"> <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&#x25cf;</p> </td>
      <td> <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt;">Level&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2&#x2014;includes</em>&#xa0;instruments for which the valuations are based upon quoted market prices in active markets involving&#xa0;similar&#xa0;assets or inputs&#xa0;other than quoted prices that are observable for the assets.&#xa0;The market inputs used to value these&#xa0;instruments&#xa0;generally consist of market yields, recently executed transactions, broker/dealer quotes or alternative pricing sources&#xa0;with&#xa0;reasonable levels&#xa0;of price transparency. Pricing sources&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> may&#xa0;</em>include industry standard data providers, security master files&#xa0;from&#xa0;large financial institutions, and other&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">third</em>-party sources used to determine a daily market value.</p> </td>
     </tr>
     <tr style="vertical-align: top;">
      <td style="width: 18pt;"> <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&#x25cf;</p> </td>
      <td> <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt;">Level&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">3&#x2014;includes</em>&#xa0;instruments for which the valuations are based on inputs that are unobservable and significant to the overall&#xa0;fair value measurement.</p> </td>
     </tr>
    </tbody>
   </table>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>

       <td style="width: 1%; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt;">&#xa0;</td>

    </table>
   </ix:nonNumeric>
   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">The fair value measurement involves the analysis of valuation techniques and evaluation of unobservable inputs commonly used by market participants to price similar instruments. Outputs from the valuation process are assessed against various market sources when they are available, including marketplace quotes, recent trades of similar illiquid securities and independent pricing services. The valuation of the auction-rate securities is subject to significant management judgment regarding projected future cash flows, which will depend on many factors, including the quality of the underlying collateral, estimated time to liquidity including potential to be called or restructured, underlying final maturity, insurance guaranty and market conditions, among others. Changes in any of the unobservable inputs used in the fair value measurement of auction-rate securities in isolation would result in a lower or higher fair value measurement. For example, an increase in the time-to-liquidity assumption or estimated discount rate would result in a lower fair value measurement.</p>
  </ix:nonNumeric>
  <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b>&#xa0;</b></p>
  <div>

   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">6.</em> </b><b>REAL ESTATE TRANSACTION</b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b>&#xa0;</b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">In&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> March 2019,&#xa0;</em>the Company completed the purchase of an office building and land located in Kirkland, Washington for $<ix:nonFraction id="c56483118" contextRef="d_2019-03-01_2019-03-31" unitRef="USD" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5" name="us-gaap:PaymentsToAcquireRealEstate" scale="6">52.9</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million in cash. The property also had&#xa0;in-place leases for a portion of the building which were assumed by the Company. The Company accounted for the purchase as an asset acquisition and capitalized $<ix:nonFraction id="c56483119" contextRef="d_2019-03-01_2019-03-31" unitRef="USD" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5" name="mpwr:RealEstateCapitalizedTransactionCosts" scale="6">0.4</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million of transaction costs.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">The purchase price allocation was as follows (in thousands):</p>

   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">The fair value of the building was determined based on the income approach, which considered the discounted cash flows and direct capitalization analysis, and the sales comparison approach. The fair value of land was determined based on the sales comparison approach. The fair value of the in-place leases was determined primarily based on the analysis of the economic benefits of certain cost savings&#xa0;attributable to the leases.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">The building is depreciated over a useful life of&#xa0;<ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2019-03-01_2019-03-31_PropertyPlantAndEquipmentByTypeAxis-BuildingMember" name="us-gaap:PropertyPlantAndEquipmentUsefulLife" id="c56483120" format="ixt-sec:duryear">40</ix:nonNumeric>&#xa0;years and the in-place leases are amortized over the average remaining lease terms of&#xa0;<ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2019-03-01_2019-03-31_FiniteLivedIntangibleAssetsByMajorClassAxis-LeasesAcquiredInPlaceMember" name="us-gaap:FiniteLivedIntangibleAssetUsefulLife" id="c56483121" format="ixt-sec:duryear">3.5</ix:nonNumeric>&#xa0;years.</p>
  </ix:nonNumeric>
  <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b>&#xa0;</b></p>
  <div>
    &#xa0;
  </div>
  <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31" name="mpwr:LeasesDisclosureTextBlock" id="c1442976" escape="true">
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">7.</em> LEASES</b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b>&#xa0;</b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;<b><i>Lessee</i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">The Company has operating leases primarily for administrative and sales and marketing offices, manufacturing operations and research and development facilities, employee housing units and certain equipment. These leases have remaining lease terms from less than a year&#xa0;to&#xa0;<span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c56483127">four</span>&#xa0;years. Some of these leases include options to renew the lease term for up to&#xa0;<span style="-sec-ix-hidden:c56483128">five</span>&#xa0;years or on a month-to-month basis. The Company does&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">not</em>&#xa0;have finance lease arrangements.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">As of&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2019,&#xa0;</em>operating lease ROU assets totaled $<ix:nonFraction id="c56483130" contextRef="i_2019-12-31_BalanceSheetLocationAxis-OtherAssetsMember" unitRef="USD" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5" name="us-gaap:OperatingLeaseRightOfUseAsset" scale="6">2.9</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million and operating lease liabilities totaled $<ix:nonFraction id="c56483131" contextRef="i_2019-12-31_BalanceSheetLocationAxis-OtherLiabilitiesMember" unitRef="USD" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5" name="us-gaap:OperatingLeaseLiability" scale="6">2.4</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million.    <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">8</em></b><b>.&#xa0;&#xa0;STOCK-BASED COMPENSATION</b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i>&#xa0;</i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i><em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em></i></b><b><i>&#xa0;Equity Incentive Plan (as amended, the&#xa0;</i></b><b>&#x201c;</b><b><i><em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em></i></b><b>&#xa0;</b><b><i>Plan&#x201d;)</i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">The Board of Directors adopted the&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em>&#xa0;Plan in&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> April 2013,&#xa0;</em>and the stockholders&#xa0;approved it in&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> June 2013.&#xa0;</em>In&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> October 2014,&#xa0;</em>the Board of Directors approved certain amendments to the&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em>&#xa0;Plan. The&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em>&#xa0;Plan, as amended, became effective on&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> November 13, 2014&#xa0;</em>and provides for the issuance of up to&#xa0;<ix:nonFraction id="c56483182" contextRef="i_2014-11-13_PlanNameAxis-The2014PlanMember" unitRef="Share" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardNumberOfSharesAuthorized" scale="6">5.5</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million shares. The&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em>&#xa0;Plan will expire on&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> November 13, 2024.&#xa0;</em>As of&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;"> December 31, 2019,&#xa0;</em><ix:nonFraction id="c56483184" contextRef="i_2019-12-31_PlanNameAxis-The2014PlanMember" unitRef="Share" format="ixt:numdotdecimal" decimals="-5" name="us-gaap:ShareBasedCompensationArrangementByShareBasedPaymentAwardNumberOfSharesAvailableForGrant" scale="6">1.6</ix:nonFraction>&#xa0;million shares remained available for future issuance under the&#xa0;<em style="font-weight: inherit; font-style: normal;">2014</em>&#xa0;Plan.&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <div class="PGBK" style="width: 100%; margin-left: 0pt; margin-right: 0pt">

   <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;"><b><i>RSUs</i></b></p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">The&#xa0;Company&#x2019;s RSUs include time-based RSUs, RSUs with performance conditions (&#x201c;PSUs&#x201d;), RSUs with market conditions (&#x201c;MSUs&#x201d;), and RSUs with both market and performance conditions (&#x201c;MPSUs&#x201d;). Vesting of awards with performance conditions or market conditions is subject to the achievement of pre-determined performance goals and the approval of such achievement by the Compensation Committee of the Board of Directors (the &#x201c;Compensation Committee&#x201d;). All awards include service conditions which require continued employment with the Company.</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">A summary of RSU activity&#xa0;is presented in the table below (in thousands, except per-share amounts):</p>
   <p style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin: 0pt; text-align: left;">&#xa0;</p>
   <ix:nonNumeric contextRef="d_2019-01-01_2019-12-31" name="us-gaap:ScheduleOfSharebasedCompensationRestrictedStockAndRestrictedStockUnitsActivityTableTextBlock" id="c1442985" escape="true">
    <table border="0" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" class="finTable" style="width: 100%; font-size: 10pt; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; text-indent: 0px;">
     <tbody>
      <tr style="vertical-align: bottom;">
       <td style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; width: 19%;">&#xa0;</td>
       <td style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; width: 1%;">&#xa0;</td>
       <td colspan="6" style="text-align: center; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom: 1px solid rgb(0, 0, 0); width: 11%;"> <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:center;"><b><b>Time-Based RSUs</b></b></p> </td>
       <td style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; padding-bottom: 1px; width: 1%;">&#xa0;</td>
       <td style="font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; width: 1%;">&#xa0;</td>
       <td colspan="6" style="text-align: center; font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;, Times, serif; font-size: 10pt; margin-left: 0pt; border-bottom: 1px solid rgb(0, 0, 0); width: 12%;"> <p style="font-family:'Times New Roman', Times, serif;font-size:10pt;margin:0pt;text-align:center;"><b><b>PSUs and MPSUs</b></b></p> </td>
